name: Dyami Brown
headline: Field-stretcher with no role: a deep-bench dart at best
body:
Brown's profile keeps screaming complementary deep threat without the volume to matter. His 2025 stint in Jacksonville produced just 37 targets across 11 games (6.8% target share), and the efficiency cratered: a minus-0.056 EPA per play, a 40.5% success rate, and five drops on only 25 catchable balls (a 13.5% drop rate). The 14.0 air yards per target confirm the vertical usage, but he turned that into 20 grabs for 227 yards and one score, finishing as the WR108.

Now back in Washington for a second tour on a one-year, $3 million deal, he projects as a depth piece behind an established receiver room. Even the rosier outside projection (roughly 30-394-3) caps him near 55 fantasy points, and his expert rank sits around WR141 with an ADP outside pick 350. Until an injury opens snaps, there's nothing here to draft. Watch the role, not the talent.
